codeReview.summary: Catalog brand/product CRUD, search/filter, and schema additions are largely implemented as required, but bulk per-item validation has a blocking gap: bean validation is bypassed because CatalogService catches generic Exception for each item, so invalid payloads return FAILED rows instead of validation errors.

issues.description: Bulk endpoint expects per-item bean validation, but method-level validation on list elements is effectively neutralized by service-level exception swallowing; invalid nested fields do not trigger request rejection/structured validation response.

bulkUpsertProducts catches ApplicationException and generic Exception per item, converting validation exceptions (including jakarta.validation.ConstraintViolationException thrown during create/update) into success=FALSE row messages instead of enforcing invalid item constraints. This violates the feature’s expected behavior for per-item validation and allows malformed records (e.g., blank product name) to be reported as ordinary FAILED entries rather than validation errors.

Bulk tests only cover one business-validation failure path (empty cartonSizes) and do not assert behavior for bean-validation failures (e.g., blank name, null brandId), which would have exposed the exception-swallowing bug in bulk processing.
Bulk per-item bean validation is bypassed due to generic exception catching in CatalogService.bulkUpsertProducts.
issues.evidence: CatalogController.java:105 uses @Valid list elements, but CatalogService.java:205-222 catches Exception for each item and maps it to FAILED result message.

issues.description: Feature expectation 'Bulk product creation with per-item validation' is not fully met for Jakarta Bean Validation constraints.
issues.evidence: features.json:1529 requires per-item validation; bulk test only exercises business rule failure, not bean-validation failures (CatalogServiceBulkUpsertTest.java:70-114).

issues.description: CatalogService exceeds mission guidance service size threshold (~500 lines).
issues.evidence: CatalogService.java is 516 lines (wc output), while AGENTS.md mandates no service class should exceed ~500 lines.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Mission guidance conflicts with backend-worker skill on whether .factory content may be edited.
sharedStateObservations.rationale: AGENTS.md says do not modify .factory contents, but backend-worker/SKILL.md mandates updating .factory/library/frontend-handoff.md for API changes; workers consistently edit this file, so guidance should be clarified to avoid ambiguity.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker procedure frequently invokes .factory/init.sh, but no setup/init command is documented in services.yaml.
sharedStateObservations.rationale: Transcript skeleton for session d55fcb5b includes executing .factory/init.sh, while services.yaml only lists compile/test/build/lint and runtime services; documenting init command would improve reproducibility.

summary: Fail: core catalog CRUD/search/mapping work is present, but bulk validation handling has a blocking correctness issue because per-item bean-validation exceptions are swallowed and downgraded to generic FAILED row messages.
